title: Graphiti
oneLiner: Temporal knowledge graph memory system for agents that need facts, entities, and time-aware context.
summary: Graphiti is an open-source temporal knowledge graph system from Zep for building agent memory that tracks entities, relationships, and changing facts over time.
whyItMatters: Graphiti matters because memory is often relational and time-sensitive. Agents need to know not only what was said, but when it was true and how it connects to other facts.
bestFor: Builders who need graph memory instead of simple chat history
Teams working with changing entities, relationships, or customer context
Developers exploring temporal memory for agent systems
notFor: Users who want a fully managed consumer product with no setup work
Teams that cannot review the linked source, license, and operational requirements before adoption
seoTitle: Graphiti: Memory Systems resource for open AI builders
seoDescription: Graphiti profile on OpenAgent.bot: what it is, when to use it, official links, open-source status, and structured resource data.
shareDescription: Temporal knowledge graph memory system for agents that need facts, entities, and time-aware context.

coreStrengths.title: Temporal knowledge graph model
coreStrengths.description: Graphiti represents memory as entities and relationships with time-aware context.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: This helps agents reason about changing facts instead of flattening everything into chunks.

coreStrengths.title: Agent memory orientation
coreStrengths.description: The project is built for agent and assistant memory workflows.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: It focuses on recall structures that matter to agents, not just generic graph storage.

coreStrengths.title: Open-source implementation
coreStrengths.description: The repository and docs provide a concrete path for evaluation.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: Graph memory is easier to trust when the implementation is inspectable.

useCaseNotes.title: Customer or user memory
useCaseNotes.description: Track evolving relationships, preferences, and events across time.

useCaseNotes.title: Research knowledge graphs
useCaseNotes.description: Represent entities and links discovered during research workflows.

useCaseNotes.title: Agent context retrieval
useCaseNotes.description: Retrieve facts by relationship and recency rather than only semantic similarity.

compareNotes.title: Graphiti is stronger for relational memory
compareNotes.summary: Vector search is useful for fuzzy recall; Graphiti is more appropriate when relationships and time matter.

commandLine.label: Clone Graphiti
commandLine.description: Use the official docs for current installation and storage configuration.

faq.question: Is Graphiti open source?
faq.answer: Graphiti is listed with Apache-2.0 based on its official source links. Always re-check the repository or model card before production use.
